---
title: "R Course Material"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### Course Title: Introduction to R for Data Analysis

#### Content

1\. Introduction to R and RStudio

-   Basics of R language
-   Understanding the RStudio environment
-   Installing and managing packages

2.  Data Types and Structures

-   Vectors, Matrices, Arrays
-   Lists and Data Frames
-   Factors

3.  Data Manipulation

-   Data import/export (csv, excel, databases)
-   Data cleaning and preprocessing
-   dplyr for data manipulation (filter, arrange, select, mutate, summarize)
-   Handling missing data

4.  Data Visualization

-   Base R plotting system
-   ggplot2 for advanced graphics
-   Interactive visualization with plotly or shiny

5.  Statistical Analysis

-   Descriptive statistics
-   Hypothesis testing
-   ANOVA, Regression Analysis
-   Time Series Analysis

6.  Advanced Data Analysis Techniques

-   Data transformation (tidyr)
-   Principal Component Analysis
-   Cluster Analysis

7.  Programming Concepts in R

-   Writing functions
-   Control structures (loops, if-else statements)
-   Error and exception handling

8.  Working with Large Datasets

-   Data.table package
-   Working with databases using R

9.  Report Generation and Reproducible Research

-   Markdown and R Markdown
-   Creating reports and presentations
-   Integrating R with LaTeX (if relevant)

10. Special Topics (based on interest and time)

-   Introduction to Machine Learning with R
-   Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
-   Spatial Data Analysis
-   Bioinformatics and R (if applicable to the student group)

11. Capstone Project

-   Applying learned skills to a real-world dataset
-   Project presentations and peer review Additional Resources and Best Practices
-   Version control with Git and GitHub
-   R community and continuing education resources Teaching Approach
-   Hands-on coding sessions
-   Case studies and real-world examples
-   Regular assignments and quizzes
-   Guest lectures from industry experts (optional)

#### Lectures Breakdown

1\. Introduction to R and RStudio (2 Lectures)

2\. Data Types and Structures (3 Lectures)

3\. Data Manipulation (4 Lectures)

4\. Data Visualization (4 Lectures)

5\. Statistical Analysis (5 Lectures)

6\. Advanced Data Analysis Techniques (4 Lectures)

7\. Programming Concepts in R (3 Lectures)

8\. Working with Large Datasets (2 Lectures)

9\. Report Generation and Reproducible Research (3 Lectures)

10\. Special Topics (5 Lectures) - This can vary depending on the topics chosen.

11\. Capstone Project (4 Lectures) - Introducing and reviewing the project.

12\. Additional Resources and Best Practices (2 Lectures)

Total: 36 Lectures

### Introduction

#### Course Overview

Welcome to "Introduction to R for Data Analysis," a course designed to equip undergraduate students with the fundamental skills required to utilize R for effective data analysis. This course offers a comprehensive introduction to R, a powerful and widely-used programming language and environment for statistical computing and graphics.

**Course Objectives:** - To familiarize students with the basics of the R programming language. - To enable students to perform data manipulation, analysis, and visualization using R. - To develop proficiency in using RStudio, the integrated development environment for R. - To teach students how to interpret data and carry out statistical tests. - To prepare students to undertake complex data analysis tasks relevant to their academic and future professional work.

**Course Goals:** - Gain an understanding of R syntax and control structures. - Learn how to import, clean, and manage datasets in R. - Develop the ability to create a variety of plots and graphs to visualize data. - Understand the application of statistical models in R for making inferences. - Complete a capstone project that applies the concepts learned to a real-world dataset.

**Why Learn R?** R is at the forefront of data analysis, used extensively in academia, research, and industry. With its vast array of packages and community support, R provides an unparalleled platform for analyzing and extracting meaningful insights from data. Learning R opens up opportunities for data-driven research and analysis across various fields such as finance, healthcare, social sciences, and more.

**Course Prerequisites:** No prior experience with R or programming is required. However, students are expected to have a basic understanding of statistics and be comfortable with mathematical concepts.

**Teaching Methodology:** The course will combine theoretical instruction with practical, hands-on sessions. Students will engage with the material through interactive lectures, in-class exercises, homework assignments, and a comprehensive project. Collaboration and discussion will be encouraged to enhance the learning experience.

**Course Materials:** - R and RStudio software (free and open-source). - Course lecture notes and slides. - Reading materials and reference books. - Online resources and tutorials. - Datasets for practice and assignments.

By the end of this course, students will be confident in their ability to utilize R to conduct a thorough data analysis process, from data import to final reporting. Join us as we embark on this journey to master the art and science of data analysis with R.
